body:
# PULL REQUEST

## Purpose and Content
This PR separates rain and snow in the precipitation cache variable and disk-saving function. 

It utilizes a simple strategy: rain if temperature above freezing and snow if below. 

## Benefits and Risks
(State concisely the benefits to be derived from and the risks associated with this PR)

- [ ] All tests ran successfully on my local machine OR N/A.
- [ ] All classes, modules, and function contain docstrings OR N/A.
- [ ] Documentation has been added/updated OR N/A.
title: Separate precipitation into rain and snow in cache

body: This PR fixes #623: save to solution and save to disk callbacks should be orthogonal. I've also added a test to make sure that this doesn't show up again.
title: Fix 623

body:
I'm feeling a bit bottlenecked by our CI times and, I'm not getting the impression that people are looking at all of the results on all runs (I typically only look at results if mse tables change). Can we shorten `t_end` on a bunch of jobs to bring CI time down to ~20 min? cc @szy21, @jiahe23.

One downside to this is that it kind of hides how fast/slow we are per job, which is useful for the performance team to understand where we are vs where we'd like to be per job. But this still may be an issue at the moment. I was thinking that we could add a sort of dummy parameter, `t_end_desired` so that we can see how long we wish each job would run for to see useful results. If we do this per buildkite job, we could write a summary afterwards so that we have a clear understanding of where, and how much, we're falling short.
title: Shorten t_end for multiple jobs to shorten CI times
